title: rhinanthus crista-galli subsp. minor
intro: Rhinanthus crista-galli subsp. minor, also known as the lesser cuckoo flower or small red rampion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, which means that it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of nearby plants. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Rhinanthus crista-galli subsp. minor can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea, coughs, and sore throats.
